skill.name: test-automator
skill.description: Guidance for designing maintainable test automation strategies, TDD workflows, CI/CD quality gates, and reporting practices.
skill.summary: Plan scalable test automation, TDD workflows, and quality engineering practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no executable code, network access, credential handling, cryptographic implementation, or reconnaissance instructions. The reported matches are natural-language Markdown guidance and filename references, so the skill is safe to publish with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Pattern
low_findings.description: Line 26 uses Markdown backticks around resources/implementation-playbook.md in an instruction to open a local Markdown reference. It does not contain shell execution syntax or a command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain instructional text with a Markdown-formatted path. No shell, subprocess, Ruby backtick execution, or user-controlled command input is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Matches
low_findings.description: The reported lines contain natural language terms such as description, desktop, and design. No cryptographic API, cipher selection, hashing logic, or security implementation appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirms these are prose references to testing concepts. I found no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak algorithm usage as executable or configuration content.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: Lines 107 and 117 discuss testing strategy and mobile testing coverage. They do not instruct collection of host, process, network, identity, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are high-level capability descriptions. No commands, APIs, or procedural steps for system discovery are present.

content.user_title: Automate Test Strategy and Quality Gates
content.value_statement: Testing teams struggle to choose durable automation patterns across unit, API, UI, and performance tests. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan test frameworks, TDD flows, CI/CD gates, and reporting practices.
actual_capabilities: Guides test-first development using red-green-refactor steps.
Recommends automation frameworks for UI, API, mobile, performance, and contract testing.
Outlines CI/CD test integration, parallel execution, and quality gate planning.
Covers test data management, privacy, mocking, and service virtualization practices.
Defines reporting, metrics, dashboards, and test effectiveness review approaches.
limitations: The skill contains guidance only and does not include executable test scripts.
It does not run tests, inspect repositories, or verify application behavior by itself.
Framework recommendations depend on project context supplied by the user.
The referenced implementation playbook is not included in the scanned file structure.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Automation Suite
use_cases.description: Define test layers, framework choices, data needs, and CI/CD integration before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Adopt TDD for Feature Work
use_cases.description: Create a red-green-refactor workflow with failing tests, minimal implementation steps, and measurable feedback loops.

use_cases.title: Improve Release Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Design smoke tests, regression checks, performance thresholds, and reporting for pipeline-based releases.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Test Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a test automation strategy for this application. Ask for missing context, then recommend test layers, tools, and first steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a TDD Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a TDD plan for this feature. Include the first failing tests, expected behavior, minimal implementation steps, and refactoring checks.

prompt_templates.title: Build CI Quality Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Design CI/CD quality gates for this repository. Include test selection, parallel execution, reporting, flaky test handling, and release criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize an Automation Program
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this test automation program and propose a modernization roadmap. Cover framework fit, coverage gaps, test data, observability, metrics, and maintenance risk.

output_examples.input: We need automated tests for a microservices platform.
output_examples.output: A layered strategy covering unit tests, contract tests, API checks, smoke tests, service virtualization, and pipeline reporting.

output_examples.input: Our UI tests are flaky and slow.
output_examples.output: A stabilization plan with selector rules, test isolation, parallelization, retry policy review, failure triage, and reporting improvements.

output_examples.input: We want to introduce TDD for a new feature.
output_examples.output: A red-green-refactor plan with initial failing scenarios, acceptance criteria, minimal implementation guidance, and refactoring checkpoints.

best_practices: Start with risk-based test priorities before selecting tools.
Keep test data, environments, and credentials separate from test logic.
Measure stability, execution time, coverage value, and defect detection trends.
anti_patterns: Automating every manual test without judging maintenance cost.
Depending on end-to-end UI tests for all release confidence.
Ignoring flaky tests while still using their results for release decisions.

faq.question: Does this skill generate runnable tests?
faq.answer: It provides plans, patterns, and guidance. Runnable tests require project-specific code and repository context.

faq.question: Which testing tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It references Playwright, Selenium, Appium, Postman, REST Assured, K6, JMeter, Pact, axe-core, and related platforms.

faq.question: Can it help with TDD?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers failing test design, red-green-refactor cycles, test doubles, property-based testing, and TDD metrics.

faq.question: Can it improve CI/CD testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help plan pipeline gates, parallel execution, dynamic test selection, smoke tests, and reporting.

faq.question: Does it handle test data privacy?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for synthetic data, anonymization, environment-specific provisioning, and secure credential practices.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is Markdown guidance with no executable code, network access, or confirmed dangerous behavior.
